Name: Non-admin users can create tenants
Description:
This indicator checks if non-admin users are allowed to create Entra tenants.

Required permissions

  • Policy.Read.All
LikelihoodOfCompromise:
By default all users are allowed to create additional Entra tenants from an existing Entra tenant. When created, the user will be invited into the new Entra tenant as a guest and will have Global Administrator access within that tenant. While the new Entra tenant will not have any resources associated with it, organizations may want to limit this from a shadow IT perspective.

If users start associating resources with this tenant, and it is not managed and monitored properly, it may become an easy target for an attacker.

References

Default user permissions - Microsoft Entra | Microsoft Learn
ResultMessage: Non-admin users are able to create Entra tenants
Remediation:
Enable Restrict non-admin users from creating tenants in the Entra tenant.

To enable, open the Microsoft Entra admin center (direct link), expand Identity, expand Users, and select User settings.

In the blade on the right, toggle Restrict non-admin users from creating tenants to yes.

Select Save at the bottom of the blade.

Organizations should understand how this change impacts their users and communicate the change out according to defined business processes.
